chapters.title: Faith gives people a way to make sense of the world
chapters.text:
Faith runs through the entire thread of human history. As far back as our cave-dwelling ancestors, we find traces of a belief system, from drawings on cave walls to early accounts of burial ceremonies. Two subjects pull humans toward faith: the wish for life after death and the wish to make sense of the world.

From unexplained weather events to the question of how we came to live on this planet, faith has offered comfort to millions of people for centuries. We have pieced together the customs and ceremonies of past civilizations from relics uncovered in excavations, which gives a fascinating glimpse into history. In many faiths, the central tenets have not changed much since their beginnings. Life has evolved, yet the basics have stayed largely in place.

Every faith believes something slightly different from the others, yet the basics are often very similar in structure.

Faith and morality are tightly bound. It is the broad idea of good versus evil, and the view that being and doing good is the path to heaven or a successful afterlife. With that in mind, many faiths guide followers toward a life that supports that aim. Morality differs from faith to faith, but every major faith has a strong focus on the afterlife.

Learning more about the main faiths around the world helps knock down walls of confusion and helps everyone become more open-minded and accepting of one another. It is also genuinely interesting to learn more about traditions you may not know well, which makes this summary an enlightening read on many levels.

chapters.title: Ancient faith still shapes the major traditions of today
chapters.text:
We tend to think about the main faiths we still see today, like Christianity, Buddhism, Hinduism, Islam, and Judaism. Yet many civilizations across ancient history had their own belief systems. Those systems helped shape societies in particular regions, such as Egypt, and connect with the major traditions we see now.

We have established that the afterlife runs through every faith like a strong thread. The Ancient Egyptians may be the most famous example of elaborate rituals to ensure passage to the afterlife. Bowing down to Osiris, Lord of the Afterlife, ancient Egyptians often made considerable offerings to the dead, including building the Great Pyramids and a series of tombs underneath. Even the rituals we read about, such as mummifying and embalming, were all to ensure safe passage to the afterlife, or Aaru.

Egyptians wanted to live after death as perfected beings in Aaru, the field of reeds, which was a perfected version of the Egypt they already knew, as Neil Philip has written.

Another historical figure who helped shape the idea of faith and passage into the afterlife was Confucius. A Chinese scholar, Confucius explored whether being a good person helped you pass into the afterlife, or whether goodness was something you were born with.

For acceptance into the afterlife, a person should focus on doing good throughout life. It should be for no benefit of their own, but simply because they can.

Confucius' idea of morality held that a person could learn to be good to ensure acceptance into the afterlife. This includes doing good deeds for others, reading and learning for no reason other than to do it, and focusing on achieving something called ren, or humaneness. Confucius taught that someone who could do these things would secure acceptance into heaven.

For reference, the Ancient Egyptians believed that every person had a shadow that helped protect them and allowed them to make good choices throughout life.

chapters.title: Hindus hold many gods yet focus on a virtuous life
chapters.text:
Hinduism can be traced back to around 1700 BCE and is built on the Four Vedas, the oldest scriptures of the tradition, written in Sanskrit. The religion grounds itself in tradition, and despite its status as a religion, it does not follow the usual rules of one. For example, Hindus do not have a single deity or god.

Hinduism can feel confusing because it covers many slightly different traditions that span across India. Many customs vary from place to place, yet the basic way people live their lives stays the same.

The word Hindu roughly translates to Indian, which, compared to other faiths, makes Hinduism more of a way of life than a traditional belief system.

Even without a named deity or prophet, Hindus follow the theory of samsara, the life cycle of birth, death, and rebirth of the soul, called atman. There is also a strong desire to attain moksha, which is release from the endless cycle of life and death, and progression to the afterlife.

As with most other traditions, to be admitted to the afterlife, you must conduct yourself in a certain way during life. Hindus call this dharma, which means living in the right way, being virtuous and appropriate. Those are loose terms and could be open to many interpretations. That is perhaps why some Hindus live in a much stricter way than others.

chapters.title: Buddha’s teachings led to the Buddhist tradition
chapters.text:
An interesting fact about Buddhism is that it did not become a religion until many years after Buddha's death. Buddha's teachings were passed down through his scholars, eventually leading followers to live their lives in pursuit of the same level of enlightenment.

Meditation is considered a strong tool to help Buddhists reach spiritual awakening and enlightenment.

Although Buddhism has a central figure to focus on, it is similar to Hinduism and tends to be more philosophical than some other established traditions, with variations spread across the Asian region. Buddha's teachings are also interesting because they were not built on a vision or message from outside but were drawn from his own experiences.

Many years after Buddha passed away, his teachings, recorded in the Tipitaka, explained how to reach a state of enlightenment and see the path toward nirvana. At that point, you are above typical human desires and attachments, two things Buddha believed blocked human happiness and satisfaction. From this, the Four Noble Truths were founded. Dukkha is the main focus of suffering. Samudaya is the cause of suffering. Nirodha is the truth of the end of suffering. Magga is the truth of the path to ending suffering.

By achieving Magga, a difficult task, followers are on their way to the Middle Way, the same path Buddha followed to total enlightenment.

Nirvana is the ultimate goal for every Buddhist, reaching spiritual enlightenment at its highest level.

chapters.title: Jews have been persecuted for years yet hold firmly to their beliefs
chapters.text:
The first thing that comes to mind for many when they think of Judaism is the Holocaust. Yet that is not the only persecution Jewish people have faced. The Jewish tradition may be the oldest and most misunderstood of all religions, dating back to around 2000 BCE.

The Hebrew Bible, often called the Torah, describes God creating the world and His connection with the Jewish people in the Canaan region. God made a promise to the prophet Abraham that He would protect the Jewish people. He said that if they obeyed Him and His wishes, including circumcision to show their obedience, He would defend those who prayed and grant them Israel.

The unique agreement the Jews hold with God gives comfort to followers every day and helps them feel protected against hostile forces.

The Torah is so revered that nobody is allowed to touch it. It is kept in a safe spot within the synagogue and read using a pointer.

There is a great deal of misunderstanding around the Jewish faith, and this has led to many persecutions over the centuries. By learning more about this ancient tradition, we can help make sure history does not repeat itself in those horrific ways. The Jewish faith has a clear base in Israel, yet the historical horrors of past centuries have led many Jews to be displaced to other regions.

Largely because of their position as displaced immigrants and their distinctive tradition, Jews have been widely persecuted throughout their history, as Neil Philip has written.

chapters.title: Christianity remains one of the most prevalent religions worldwide
chapters.text:
Christianity dates back to around the 1st century BCE. Christians believe in one God and in the Messiah, Jesus, who died on the cross and was resurrected to heaven, bringing salvation to humankind through His sacrifice.

In the Bible, Jesus is the Son of God in human form, and when He came to earth, it marked the start of the New Testament. Most of the Christian faith is built on the teachings of Jesus throughout the Gospels and places strong emphasis on the importance of living a good life in order to be admitted to heaven.

Perhaps the most important story in the Bible is the crucifixion and Jesus' resurrection to heaven to be seated alongside God. The most important part of this story also refers to the Trinity. God exists in three ways: the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.

The Roman Catholic religion is a branch of Christianity. Catholics are Christians. The other two branches of Christianity include Protestant and Orthodox. Within those, you also find different sub-branches, usually by country.

Catholic followers are Christian, but not all Christians are Catholic.

One difference between Christianity and many other religions is that Jesus is considered to be more than a prophet. In many other religions, Jesus is not seen as the son of God.

Christianity has helped shape a great deal of western culture, even though it began in Roman Judea.

chapters.title: The teachings of God as revealed in the Qur’an
chapters.text:
Islam is an ancient religion founded in 610 CE. Like several other world faiths, it is highly misunderstood. Muslims live their lives focused on doing good for others and committing themselves to God.

One reason Islam is so misunderstood is that many have taken the original teachings and twisted them into something far removed from what is actually contained within the Qur'an, the Islamic holy book. To better understand Islam, we need to set aside those preconceived notions and get back to basics, understanding the reality of daily life as a Muslim.

What we see reported in the newspapers about Islam often misses the mark. It is essential to truly understand the religion and show respect to its followers.

Muslims believe that the Prophet Muhammad received the revelations directly from God to be recorded in the Qur'an. This is considered the true message from Allah, or God, and was written in Arabic.

Islam teaches belief in one God, and Muslims dedicate their lives to serving Allah. Life is considered a gift from God and should be lived in the right way, avoiding sin and temptation. On the Day of Judgement, it is how a person lived their life that determines whether they will go to heaven or hell.

The holiest city in Islam is Mecca, in Saudi Arabia, the birthplace of the Prophet Muhammad. Many Muslims spend their days in and around the local community mosque there. The Five Pillars of Islam are Shahada, the profession of faith. Salat is prayer. Zakat is almsgiving or offering worship to Allah. Sawm is fasting. Hajj is a pilgrimage to Mecca to be closer to Allah.

chapters.title: Closing thought
chapters.text:
No religion is better or more important than another. All religions are equal. That is something we all need to understand from the start. What one person chooses to believe and how they choose to live their life is their personal choice. We should respect individual choices and learn to live side by side in harmony.

That would be a perfect world, would it not.

Unfortunately, religion has caused divisions throughout history and even continues to do so in the modern world. The key to breaking down those walls is to understand religions and their true nature. When you only see small snapshots of what a religion is supposed to be about, it is easy to form a quick opinion. Yet it is vital to learn more and understand the facts.

Islam is one of the most misunderstood religions, alongside Judaism. Yet there are countless myths and false rumors about all the others too. Religion remains a dividing subject, even though it is designed to bring people together. If we want to live in a harmonious world, we need to understand that the religion a person chooses and how they decide to live their lives is what brings them comfort and reassurance in this challenging world we live in. It is nobody else's business to judge or pass an opinion.

Of course, religion as a subject is fascinating too. Perhaps by reading about different religions, you may decide to dig a little deeper and learn even more. Religion is not for everyone, but it is often most crucial for those who choose to live a religious life.

Try this. If you want to learn more about a religion that a friend or colleague follows, it is fine to ask questions as long as you do it respectfully. Ask if they mind and then have a deep conversation to learn even more. Cast aside any preconceived ideas you may have about a particular religion and see it for what it is, a personal choice that someone has made to live their life a certain way. Explore your own ideas and thoughts on religion and see if they have changed after spending time with this summary.

Read it till the end.
